intro: Ceradenia podocarpa, also known as "Podocarpa Fern," is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceradenia podocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
